listings.hours: Open daily except for 25 December and 1 January; 09:00-17:00
listings.content: The cemetery is the final resting place for 4,402 American military dead lost during the capture of [[Rome]] and the battle for the Apennines. A monument is inscribed with the names of 1,409 Americans whose remains were never found or identified. The atrium of the chapel contains marble maps of World War II Italy campaign.

listings.name: Gardens of Pratolino
listings.content: A park containing 14-m-high colossus sculpture

listings.name: Frank Lloyd Wright Rosenbaum House Museum
listings.content: A house built in 1939 and designed by Frank Lloyd Wright. The house is open for tours, which last about 45 minutes.

listings.name: The Florence Indian Mound & Museum
listings.content: The Tennessee Valley area's largest domiciliary mound. Features typical workmanship of the Indians who lived in this area before the Cherokees, Chickasaws, and Creeks.

listings.name: The Kennedy-Douglass Center for the Arts
listings.content: Showcases artists from around the Southeast, and offers classes and workshops to people of all ages. It is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.

listings.name: Pope's Tavern
listings.content: This building served as a hospital along the way of many Civil War soldiers from both the North and South. It also served time as a stagecoach stop, a tavern, and an inn. The museum houses Civil War artifacts as well as antiques from the 18th and 19th centuries. This remains one of Florence's oldest standing structures.

listings.name: Children's Museum of the Shoals
listings.content: Contains exhibits displaying the history, people, and events that make up the Shoals' history. The museum is designed to promote learning in a hands-on environment. The museum offers educational workshops year-round for children of all ages.

listings.name: The Forks of Cypress
listings.image: Forks of Cypress Ruins by Highsmith 02.jpg
listings.content: This was a cotton plantation located in Florence. The remains can still be seen in the form of 24 Greek columns and the Jackson Family cemetery.

listings.name: Wilson Dam
listings.content: This 110x600 feet Dam is part of the TVA system. The maximum lift is 100 feet, it is the highest single lift lock east of the Rockies.

listings.name: Wichahpi Commemorative Stone Wall
listings.content: Trail of Tears memorial, built by a descendant of one who made the journey.

listings.name: Sacred Way Sanctuary
listings.content: Sanctuary for Indigenous Native American horses.

listings.name: Muscle Shoals Sound Studios
listings.content: Visit an important site of musical history in the US where notorieties like Bob Dylan and The Rolling Stones recorded albums, as was popularized in the film Muscle Shoals.

listings.name: Fame Recording Studios
listings.content: Another very eventful recording studio for the development of rock and blues American musical styles of the 20th Century, where such likenesses as Aretha Franklin debuted. Take a tour.

listings.name: W.C. Handy Festival
listings.content: Enjoy jazz and blues as Florence celebrates its native son.

listings.name: Florence Renaissance Faire
listings.hours: fourth Saturday and Sunday in October
listings.content: Held in Wilson Park this unusual event draws 30,000 – 40,000 people each year to its site in downtown Florence, Alabama. It is always held on the fourth Saturday and Sunday in October, taking advantage of the fall weather and foliage. Wilson Park hosts a variety of vendors, events, and demonstrations with a medevile theme.

listings.name: The Sam Phillips Music Celebration
listings.alt: started January 2005. It is an annual week long event that takes place the first week in January by celebrated with events that include the Sam Phillips Birthday Party

listings.name: The George Lindsey/UNA Film Festival
listings.alt: is named in honor of Florence native, George Lindsey Lindsey is an actor who is most famous portrayal of Goober Pyle on The Andy Griffith Show. Lindsey is a UNA - known then as Florence State College - graduate.

listings.name: Arts Alive
listings.alt: began in 1986 and celebrates the art of Southern artist who gather to display their work in Wilson Park

listings.name: The Spirit of Freedom Celebration
listings.alt: showcases music, and fireworks to celebrate Independence Day on the banks of the Tennessee River

listings.name: Trail of Tears Commemorative Motorcycle Ride
listings.content: Commemorates the final point at which area Cherokee Native Americans were shipped off to Oklahoma and the mid-west by the Indian Removal Act of 1830.

listings.name: First Fridays in Florence
listings.alt: arts and music event that occurs every First Friday from April through December in Historic Downtown Florence.

listings.name: Florence Mall
listings.content: Regional Mall consisting of 4 main Anchors, Sears, JC Penney, Dillards and Belks. Department Stores. Contains most mid price national chains and is the focal point for surrounding shopping centers and big box stores.

listings.name: 360 Grille and Lounge
listings.content: Alabama's only revolving restaurant overlooks the city and Tennessee River.

listings.name: Renaissance Shoals Resort & Spa
listings.content: Offers high-speed internet access, private balconies with river views, two championship golf courses on The Robert Trent Jones Golf Trail, and a 6,000-square-foot European style spa.

listings.name: Florence Y'All Water Tower
listings.content: If you're heading south from Ohio, this is a surefire sign that you aren't in the Union anymore. Originally a giant advertisement for the nearby Florence Mall, this landmark was accidentally created when developers realized that it was illegal to advertise the mall on the water tower. To save money they turned the M to a Y and the rest is history.

listings.name: Florence Y'alls
listings.directions: near the intersection of I-75 and State Route 42; take exit #180 off I-75 and proceed east one block; turn right on Freedom Way
listings.hours: The front office and the Truist Team Shop remain open all year long during normal business hours
listings.price: VIP $10.00, reserved $8.50, lawn $6.00, children 2 and under are free
listings.content: The Florence Y'alls, named after the water tower, are a professional baseball team based in Florence. They play in the West Division of the Frontier League, which is not directly affiliated with Major League Baseball but became an "MLB Partner League" when MLB reorganized the minors in 2021. From the 2004 season to the present, the Y'alls have played their home games at Thomas More Stadium, which is located near Interstates 71 and 75. The ballpark is also home to the baseball team of nearby Thomas More University, which bought the right to name the park in 2022.

listings.name: Florence Mall
listings.content: Anchors are JC Penny and Macy's.

listings.name: Lita's Tacos
listings.content: Authentic, street-style tacos, nachos, and other Mexican cuisine - but the tacos are the real stars of the show. Super cheap and located right next to a gas station and convenience store - and mind-shatteringly delicious. The owners are chill, too!

listings.name: Comfort Suites Florence - Cincinnati South
listings.content: Indoor pool and gym.

listings.name: Courtyard Florence Greater Cincinnati Area
listings.content: Little fire pit to gather around for socializing.

listings.name: Holiday Inn Express & Suites Florence - Cincinnati Airport
listings.content: Also sporting an indoor pool.

listings.name: Sea Lion Caves
listings.directions: 11 miles North of Florence on the Oregon Coast
listings.content: The world's largest sea cave is home to many wild sea lions and bird species, all struggling with the sea for a spot on their favorite rock. Here you can take an elevator down to sea level to catch a glimpse inside the cave.

listings.name: Old Town
listings.content: Florence's historic waterfront.

listings.name: Jessie M. Honeyman Memorial State Park
Rhododendron Festival
listings.content: Florence's annual festival every May, popular with bikers.

listings.name: Three Rivers Casino Resort
listings.content: Slots, live and electronic table games, poker room, bingo and keno. Also has a sportsbook near restaurants  and a cocktail bar.

listings.name: Blue Dolphin Antique and Collectables
listings.content: New, vintage, and antique items from multiple vendors.

listings.name: Mo's
listings.content: Fish & Clamchowder restaurant in Florence's old town. Great view of the Siuslaw river and one of the best clam chowders to be found on the Oregon coast. This local chain of restaurant's was started in Newport, Oregon.

listings.name: Siuslaw River Coffee Roasters
listings.content: With a river deck, almost underneath the bridge, has friendly staff, excellent pastries, and a wide-ranging selection of coffee.

listings.name: Best Western Pier Point Inn
listings.content: 85625 Highway 101.

listings.name: Landmark Inn
listings.content: Stylish Bed and Breakfast with an outstanding (literally) site just above the old town but also handy for the newer business district and local facilities such as the hospital

listings.name: Harbor Vista County Campground and Park
KCST 106.9FM & KCFM 104.1FM, 1250AM - Coast Radio Florence
listings.content: Local News, Sports and Everything Florence.

listings.name: KXCR 90.7FM - Oregon Coast Community Radio
listings.content: Florence Area Community Radio on the Central Oregon Coast.

listings.name: PDRTA Florence Transit
listings.content: Offers 7 fixed-route bus services in the city.

listings.name: Florence County Museum
listings.content: Art and local history. Antiques, paintings, Civil War, South West American Indian exhibits and more.

listings.name: Florence Stockade Visitor Center
listings.content: Between 1864 and 1865, about 16,000 Union soldiers were held captive here, in abysmal conditions that consummated in the deaths of about 2,800 of these men. The site is not as developed as Andersonville State Historic Park is in Georgia today, but it still offers opportunity for a reflective mood.

listings.name: Florence National Cemetery
listings.content: Next to the stockade, this is where a lot of the POW camp casualties ended up.

listings.name: Magnolia Mall Shopping Center
listings.content: Many well known stores inside such as Best Buy, Sunglass Hut, Express, JC Penney, Foot Locker, Barnes & Nobles, etc.

listings.name: Red Bone Alley Restaurant and Bar
listings.content: Southern food restaurant with weekly entertainment and reasonable prices.

listings.name: Quincy's Family SteakHouse
listings.content: You can really chow down here at this American buffet.

listings.name: Woodstone BBQ and Seafood
listings.content: BBQ and seafood buffet.

listings.name: Howard Johnson by Wyndham Florence
listings.content: Pool and light breakfast.

listings.name: Abingdon Manor Inn & Restaurant
listings.content: Charming southern inn. Multi-course prix-fixe dinner served for $65. Rooms have a private bath, TV, and wifi.

listings.name: ITA Airways
listings.content: Flag carrier and national airline of Italy. It's part of the SkyTeam alliance, and also codeshares with other carriers outside the alliance. Rome Fiumicino ({{IATA|FCO}}) is the main hub, while Milano Malpensa ({{IATA|MXP}}) has been relegated to a lesser role.

listings.name: Ryanair
listings.content: Many destinations in Italy.

listings.name: easyjet
listings.content: Many destinations in Italy.

listings.name: Wizz Air
listings.content: Connects Italy to almost all parts of Europe as well as to the Southern Caucasus, Northern Africa and the Middle East like the UAE.

listings.name: Italo
listings.content: ".italo" high-speed trains serve major cities. It is a luxurious service, and for some routes and dates, their prices are lower than the competition's.

listings.name: Trenitalia
listings.content:
Trenitalia runs a wide range of train types: '''[[high-speed trains]]''' (Frecciarossa, Frecciargento, Frecciabianca), '''Intercity''', '''regional trains''' (Regionali, Regionali Veloci) and '''international trains''' (Eurocity, Euronight).
High-speed trains are very comfortable, travelling up to 360km/h and stopping only at major stations and connect only the main cities. They charge a supplement to the standard ticket, which includes the booking fee. Regional trains are the slowest, cheapest and least reliable, stopping at all stations. Intercity trains are somewhere between high-speed and local trains. They are generally reliable.

listings.name: Uffizi Museum
listings.content: One of the greatest museums in the world and a must-see. Given the great number of visitors, advance ticket reservation is a good idea, to avoid hour-long queues.

listings.name: Galleria dell'Accademia
listings.content: Home to Michelangelo's famous statue of David.

listings.name: Brera Art Gallery
listings.content: A prestigious museum in a fine 17th-century palace, which boasts several paintings, including notable ones from the Renaissance era.

listings.name: The Etruscan Academy Museum of the City of Cortona
listings.directions: [[Cortona#Museum of the Etruscan Academy and the City of Cortona|Cortona]], Tuscany

listings.name: Egyptian Museum
listings.content: Holds the second-largest Egyptian collection in the world, after Egypt's Cairo Museum collection.

listings.name: The Aquarium
listings.content: One of the largest and most beautiful in the world, in the '''Porto Antico''' (ancient port) in an area completely renewed by architect Renzo Piano in 1992.

listings.name: Science and Technology Museum
listings.content: One of the largest in Europe, holds collections about boats, aeroplanes, trains, cars, motorcycles, radio and energy. Has also acquired the Toti submarine, which is open to visitors.

listings.name: Roman Civilization Museum
listings.content: Holds the world's largest collection about ancient Rome and a marvellous reproduction (scale 1:250) of the entire Rome area in AD 325, the age of Constantine the Great.

listings.name: National Cinema Museum
listings.content: In the historic '''Mole Antonelliana''' building, the symbol of the city.

listings.name: Automobile Museum
listings.content: One of the largest in the world, with a 170-car collection covering the entire history of automobiles.

listings.name: Capitoline Museums
listings.content: Large collections of artworks and archaeological findings from the Roman period to the Renaissance. The oldest public art museum in the world.

listings.name: The Vatican Museums
listings.content: Visit the 54 "galleries" of the museums to see the Sistine Chapel, the rooms painted by Raphael, some amazing early maps, and artwork across the centuries, mostly Christian in focus.

listings.name: Etruscan Museum
listings.content: Amazing collection of Etruscan art.

listings.name: The National Park of the Tusco-Emilian Apennines
listings.content: extends lengthwise for about 60 km from the high valleys of the mountain torrents of Parma and Baganza up to the Passo delle Forbici, opening up to include on the Tuscan side the calcareous massif of the Pania di Corfino, and in Emilia the chain of the Alps of Succiso, of Monte Cusna, the valley of the river Secchia and the isolated range of the Pietra di Bismantova.

listings.name: The National Park of the Tuscan Archipelago
listings.content: the Park was established in 1996 and it covers an area of over 18,000 hectares in addition to 40,000 hectares of sea. It is managed by an organization with the same name, that has its headquarters in Portoferraio (on Elba Island). The Park falls under the jurisdiction of the Province of [[Livorno]] and that of [[Grosseto]].

listings.name: The National Park of the Foreste Casentinesi, Monte Falterona and Campigna
listings.content: Following the Arno upstream you enter the district of Casentino surrounded by mountains to the north and the east. Here the National Park of the Foreste Casentinesi, Monte Falterona and Campigna offers a uniquely moving and unforgettable experience: that of discovering one of the oldest forests in Europe.

listings.name: The Maremma Regional Park
listings.content: the Maremma Regional Park (Parco Regionale della Maremma), also known as ''Uccellina Park'' (Parco dell’Uccellina) covers a coastal area between Principina a Mare and Talamone near [[Grosseto]], Magliano in Toscana and [[Orbetello]], right up to the [[Livorno]] – [[Rome]] train line.

listings.name: The Park of Migliarino, San Rossore and Massaciuccoli
listings.content: the Park of Migliarino, San Rossore and Massaciuccoli was established in 1975 and covers 24,000 hectares between [[Pisa]], [[Viareggio]], San Giuliano Terme, Vecchiano and Massarossa. What makes this park so special is what lies around its borders: the Tirrenian Sea, Lake Massaciuccoli and the rivers Arno, Serchio, Canale dei Navicelli and Morto e Burlamacca.

listings.name: The Montioni nature park
listings.content: managed by the Municipale Administrations of [[Grosseto]] and [[Livorno]]. Park status from 1998. The park extends over 7000 hectares and rises to 300m at Poggio al Checco, its highest point. The territory has a large artistic and culture heritage, from ancient archeological finds to Etruscan and Roman remains which have been found under medieval constructions such as the Pievaccia, the ruins of Montioni Vecchio Castle and Montioni Thermal Baths.

listings.name: The Livorno Hills Park
listings.content: the Livorno Hills Park encompasses a vast area between the districts of [[Livorno]], Collesalvetti and Rosignano Marittimo. It’s nickname is ‘the lost island’ due to the fact that this stretch of land was an island until it attached itself to the mainland thousands of years ago. The park has not only areas of outstanding natural beauty but also but has also been subject to interesting archeological, artistic and cultural discoveries.

listings.name: The Archaeological Park of Poggibonsi
listings.content: the visit starts with a short documentary film that illustrates the results of twelve years of excavation and the most important archaeological, architectonical and naturalistic aspects of the Poggio Imperiale site.

listings.name: The Parks of the Val di Cornia
listings.content: the Parks of the Val di Cornia, in Tuscany, tell a thousand-year-old story which begins with the Etruscan people and bears witness to centuries of extraction and working on metals, proposing also splendid natural, coastal and hilly environments. The system includes 2 Archaeological Parks, Natural Parks, 3 Museums, 1 Documentation Centre, included in the area of the five municipalities at the extreme south of the province of [[Livorno]], opposite the Island of Elba.

listings.name: The Zoological Park of European Fauna in Poppi
listings.content: this is the first and only park dedicated to European Fauna open in the municipality of Poppi ([[Arezzo]]).

listings.name: The Pinocchio's Park
listings.content: : Pinocchio’s Park is in [[Collodi]], lovely ancient village that has remained virtually unchanged since the last century. Its charming collection of houses, nestled among the hills, leads the way to Villa Garzoni and its lovely 19th century garden, often considered among the most beautiful in Europe.

listings.name: Medici Villas and Gardens in Tuscany
listings.content: A [[UNESCO World Heritage site]] consisting of 16 major and 11 minor villas owned by the powerful Medici family in the 15th-17th centuries.

listings.name: Maritime Bus
listings.content: Operates intercity buses in [[New Brunswick]], [[Nova Scotia]], and [[Prince Edward Island]].

listings.name: Shogomoc Historical Railway Site
listings.directions: Exit 153 on TCH, follow Hwy 105 River Valley Scenic Drive
listings.content: Museum showcases a restored Canadian Pacific Railway station and three CPR cars.

listings.name: The Florenceville Bridge
listings.content: A wooden covered bridge combined with a steel trusses which crosses the Saint John River at Florenceville. The 46.9-metre (154-foot) bridge was built in 1907.

listings.name: Replica Noah's Ark
listings.content: A 2/3-scale model of the Biblical Ark that Noah is said to have built, as described in the Old Testament. The replica ark contains the offices and student dorms for a private Bible school, the School of the Spirit, run by the Burnham Road Ministries. The sight of a 300-foot boat in the middle of potato fields within sight of Bristol and Oakland Mountain often attracts curious visitors. It's just outside the town limits.

listings.name: Festival of Flavour
listings.content: Fourdays of wine seminars, beer tastings, cooking demonstrations and delicious foods.

listings.name: National French Fry Day
listings.content: French fry cutting competition, music, food, children's activities, free French fries.

listings.name: Snow Blast
listings.content: A day of winter activities for adults and children.

listings.name: Outdoor Summer Market
listings.content: Food, produce, craft, woodworking, flower, baked goods, local meat and jewellery vendors. Each week showcases a different musical performance from local entertainers.

listings.name: Potato World
listings.content: Gourmet French fry cafe and potato museum (summer only)

listings.name: Fresh
listings.content: It's in an elegant 26-seat rail car.

listings.name: Lai Restaurant
listings.content: Chinese restaurant.

listings.name: French Fry Hut
listings.content: Fries, burgers, poutine.

listings.name: A.C. Sharkey's Pub
listings.price: Pastas, wraps, burgers, salads and mains $11-27

listings.name: Florenceville Inn
listings.content: Free Wi-Fi, air conditioning, continental breakfast, indoor heated swimming pool, guest laundry, and self-service business centre. Restaurant and lounge.

listings.name: Tannaghtyn Bed & Breakfast
listings.content: Canada Select 4 Star.

listings.name: Shamrock Suite
listings.content: A 19th-century home conversion on six acres of manicured grounds, accented by unique heritage gardens, in a quiet residential neighbourhood. Rooms available on the first floor for those not wanting to climb stairs. Private bath; continental breakfast. Laundry and shared kitchen.

listings.name: Shamrock Train Inn
listings.content: Stay in one of two authentic train cars at the Shogomoc Railway Site in downtown Florenceville-Bristol. Electric fireplace and seating areas, en suite washroom, a kitchenette with continental breakfast, Wi-Fi (no television).
